content.text: AG-UI Protocol is an open standard that defines the event vocabulary an AI agent sends to a frontend over a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream. Unlike ad-hoc SSE formats where every team invents its own event names, AG-UI gives every event a defined type, a fixed set of fields, and a precise lifecycle — so any compliant server can talk to any compliant client. The protocol is framework-agnostic: the server can be FastAPI, Express, or a Lambda; the client can be React, Vue, or plain JavaScript.

attrs.alt: AG-UI event stream: agent server emits typed events (RUN_STARTED, TEXT_MESSAGE_DELTA, TOOL_CALL_START, STATE_SNAPSHOT, RUN_FINISHED) consumed by frontend state reducer

content.text: . Additional fields depend on the event type. The full set:
When a new assistant text block opens
When the model begins a tool call
Each partial JSON chunk of tool arguments
When tool argument JSON is complete
When the tool call block closes
After run completes; replaces all local state
A thin dataclass and a single
helper serialize events as SSE lines. All events share the same dataclass; unused fields are omitted from the JSON payload.

from enum import StrEnum
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any

class AGUIEventType(StrEnum):
    RUN_STARTED          = "RUN_STARTED"
    RUN_FINISHED         = "RUN_FINISHED"
    RUN_ERROR            = "RUN_ERROR"
    TEXT_MESSAGE_START   = "TEXT_MESSAGE_START"
    TEXT_MESSAGE_DELTA   = "TEXT_MESSAGE_DELTA"
    TEXT_MESSAGE_END     = "TEXT_MESSAGE_END"
    TOOL_CALL_START      = "TOOL_CALL_START"
    TOOL_CALL_ARGS_DELTA = "TOOL_CALL_ARGS_DELTA"
    TOOL_CALL_ARGS_END   = "TOOL_CALL_ARGS_END"
    TOOL_CALL_END        = "TOOL_CALL_END"
    STATE_SNAPSHOT       = "STATE_SNAPSHOT"
    STATE_DELTA          = "STATE_DELTA"

@dataclass
class AGUIEvent:
    type: AGUIEventType
    # Each event type carries a subset of these fields:
    run_id:       str            = ""
    message_id:   str            = ""
    tool_call_id: str            = ""
    tool_name:    str            = ""
    delta:        str            = ""   # text chunk or JSON Patch ops (JSON string)
    state:        dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    error:        str            = ""

def emit(event: AGUIEvent) -> str:
    """Serialise one AG-UI event as an SSE data line."""
    import json
    payload = {k: v for k, v in vars(event).items() if v not in ("", {}, [])}
    return f"data: {json.dumps(payload)}\n\n"
The server maps Anthropic streaming SDK events to AG-UI events. One SSE endpoint handles the entire run lifecycle from

import uuid
import anthropic
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel
from ag_ui import AGUIEvent, AGUIEventType, emit   # local module from above

app    = FastAPI()
client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()

class RunRequest(BaseModel):
    messages:  list[dict]
    tools:     list[dict] = []
    system:    str = ""

@app.post("/agent/run")
async def run_agent(req: RunRequest):
    run_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

    async def stream():
        # 1. Announce the run
        yield emit(AGUIEvent(type=AGUIEventType.RUN_STARTED, run_id=run_id))

        msg_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        tool_buffers: dict[int, dict] = {}    # index -> {tool_call_id, name, raw_args}

        async with client.messages.stream(
            model="claude-opus-4-8",
            max_tokens=4096,
            system=req.system,
            messages=req.messages,
            tools=req.tools,
        ) as s:
            async for event in s:
                etype = event.type

                if etype == "content_block_start":
                    blk = event.content_block
                    if blk.type == "text":
                        yield emit(AGUIEvent(
                            type=AGUIEventType.TEXT_MESSAGE_START,
                            run_id=run_id, message_id=msg_id,
                        ))
                    elif blk.type == "tool_use":
                        tool_buffers[event.index] = {
                            "id": blk.id, "name": blk.name, "raw": ""
                        }
                        yield emit(AGUIEvent(
                            type=AGUIEventType.TOOL_CALL_START,
                            run_id=run_id,
                            tool_call_id=blk.id,
                            tool_name=blk.name,
                        ))

                elif etype == "content_block_delta":
                    d = event.delta
                    if d.type == "text_delta":
                        yield emit(AGUIEvent(
                            type=AGUIEventType.TEXT_MESSAGE_DELTA,
                            run_id=run_id, message_id=msg_id, delta=d.text,
                        ))
                    elif d.type == "input_json_delta":
                        buf = tool_buffers.get(event.index)
                        if buf:
                            buf["raw"] += d.partial_json
                            yield emit(AGUIEvent(
                                type=AGUIEventType.TOOL_CALL_ARGS_DELTA,
                                run_id=run_id,
                                tool_call_id=buf["id"],
                                delta=d.partial_json,
                            ))

                elif etype == "content_block_stop":
                    buf = tool_buffers.pop(event.index, None)
                    if buf:
                        yield emit(AGUIEvent(
                            type=AGUIEventType.TOOL_CALL_ARGS_END,
                            run_id=run_id, tool_call_id=buf["id"],
                        ))
                        yield emit(AGUIEvent(
                            type=AGUIEventType.TOOL_CALL_END,
                            run_id=run_id, tool_call_id=buf["id"],
                        ))
                    else:
                        yield emit(AGUIEvent(
                            type=AGUIEventType.TEXT_MESSAGE_END,
                            run_id=run_id, message_id=msg_id,
                        ))

        # 2. Send agent state snapshot at the end of the run
        yield emit(AGUIEvent(
            type=AGUIEventType.STATE_SNAPSHOT,
            run_id=run_id,
            state={"status": "complete", "run_id": run_id},
        ))
        yield emit(AGUIEvent(type=AGUIEventType.RUN_FINISHED, run_id=run_id))

    return StreamingResponse(stream(), media_type="text/event-stream",
                             headers={"Cache-Control": "no-cache", "X-Accel-Buffering": "no"})
The client processes AG-UI events through a pure reducer function. Each event type mutates a specific slice of the
object; the component always sees a consistent snapshot. The
function opens the fetch stream, parses SSE lines, and dispatches state updates.
At run end, the agent emits a
containing its full internal state (task list, memory, tool outputs). For long-running agents that emit many intermediate state changes,
sends only the changed fields as RFC 6902 JSON Patch operations — reducing bandwidth when the state object is large.
hook to give any component clean access to run state. The hook resets state on each new run and cleans up the fetch stream on unmount.
AG-UI vs Raw SSE vs WebSocket
If the agent throws an exception, emit
before closing the stream. The client transitions to the

# In the stream() generator, wrap the Anthropic call:
try:
    async with client.messages.stream(...) as s:
        async for event in s:
            ...  # emit events as above
except anthropic.APIError as e:
    yield emit(AGUIEvent(
        type=AGUIEventType.RUN_ERROR,
        run_id=run_id,
        error=f"{type(e).__name__}: {e.message}",
    ))
    return

# After the try/except block, emit RUN_FINISHED as normal
yield emit(AGUIEvent(type=AGUIEventType.RUN_FINISHED, run_id=run_id))

AG-UI Protocol standardises the SSE event vocabulary between an AI agent server and a frontend client. By defining typed events for text streaming, tool call lifecycles, state snapshots, and run boundaries, it makes any compliant server interoperable with any compliant client — and eliminates the per-project work of designing and documenting a custom event format. The key implementation steps are: emit
first, map Anthropic SDK streaming events to AG-UI event types, close with
